## Amazon and Two Unnamed E-Commerce Peers Face EU Retail Regulatory Crackdown

EU retail enforcement targeting AMZN adds to a broadening regulatory agenda beyond the company's prior antitrust exposure; the risk for a tech PM is incremental compliance cost or structural remedies on platform take rates in Europe, which would disproportionately affect high-margin third-party seller services.

- European regulators are targeting AMZN and two unspecified e-commerce companies in a retail enforcement action.
- The nature, scope, and potential remedies of the crackdown are not detailed in the headline.
- Coverage does not name the two peer companies subject to the action.
- The action adds to existing regulatory overhang on AMZN's platform business.
